$schema: Named steps the query_data flow performs to answer cell-level questions.

units.name: load_sheets
units.instruction: Load all sheets from the user's uploaded workbook (cache via state.all_sheets when possible).

units.name: build_schema_context
units.instruction: Build a compact schema description: sheet names + columns + 3-row preview, capped to ~800 chars per sheet.

units.name: generate_pandas
units.instruction: Ask the code-generation LLM to write a pandas/numpy snippet that answers the user's question. Final answer must be assigned to `answer`. No file I/O, no imports beyond pd/np.

units.name: review_code
units.instruction: CodeReviewerAgent critiques the candidate code (correctness, schema match, robustness). Returns {approved, severity, issues, suggestion}.

units.name: safe_execute
units.instruction: Execute the approved code in the safe_exec sandbox (restricted builtins, thread-based 15s timeout).

units.name: revise_on_failure
units.instruction: If code review rejects (major/fatal) OR sandbox raises, feed the error/critique back to the generator LLM and retry. Up to 2 revisions before giving up.

units.name: natural_language_response
units.instruction: Translate the pandas result into a clear 2-5 sentence answer. Quote exact numbers, never fabricate.
